SOUNDNESS_CORRECTNESS.justification: The paper provides empirical evidence for several claims (e.g. ResCeption ILSVRC results with best validation top-1 23.37% and top-5 6.17% in Fig. 5, MS-COCO CIDEr-D 0.923/0.937, Holidays mAP 0.783 and UKB precision/recall ≈0.907 in Sec. 3.5), and describes dataset sizes and splits (Sec. 2, Sec. 3.2). However, several methodological details are missing or informal: the residual derivation (Eq. 1–3) is hand-wavy rather than rigorous, key quantitative results referenced in Table 2/3 are not shown in the provided text, and there is little discussion of baselines, ablation studies, or statistical significance for the proposed RNN-based multi-label approach and guided ROI/detector choices. Overall the experiments support the system-level claims but there are notable gaps in comparisons and analysis that limit full technical validation.

ORIGINALITY.justification: The paper combines known components (inception-v3 with residual shortcuts, Faster R-CNN, LSTM sequence models, inverted index, CNN features) into a practical large-scale fashion search system, and claims the application of LSTM to multi-label fashion-attributes in a commercial setting. Many individual ideas are incremental or have close precedents (the authors note similar findings in Szegedy et al. and related RNN-label dependency work), so the contribution is an applied integration and engineering effort rather than a strongly novel algorithmic advance. This yields moderate novelty but limited methodological novelty for an expert reader.

CLARITY.justification: The paper follows a conventional organization (intro, dataset, system, experiments, conclusion) and references figures/tables throughout (e.g. Fig. 3, Fig. 5, Table 1–3), but the provided text contains many repetitions, formatting/LaTeX artifacts, and corrupted paragraphs which make reading and following arguments difficult. Notation is occasionally informal (e.g. mixed indexing in Eq. 3) and several referenced results (Tables/Figs) are not present in the text excerpt, reducing reproducibility and clarity of presentation.
